name: Herb Jeffries
abstract: Herb Jeffries (born Umberto Alexander Valentino; September 24, 1913 – May 25, 2014) was an American actor of film and television and popular music and jazz singer-songwriter, known for his baritone voice. He starred in several low-budget "race" Western feature films aimed at black audiences, Harlem on the Prairie (1937), Two-Gun Man from…

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Herb said, “Gerald, put a band together for me.” He was gonna front the band and everyting. I say, “OK fine, that’s good.” So I got the band together, selected all of the people who would be in the band, and then I rehearsed them
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: When I was with Earl Hines we had two singers. The first one was Herb Jeffries.

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Herb Jeffries, the singer, had been with Duke Ellington and Earl Hines. He wanted to get a band... So I was doing it for the money of course. And at the last minute, I got the band together, picked the people, put them together, Herb’s going to front the band, rehearsed them and everything, and Herb
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote 'Perdido' and 'Jeffries' was next to me in the seat in the coach, and I started singing this thing to him and he started singing it, too, and I gave it to Duke.

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: So with the manager -- we had a little manager, Sam Flaschnik was his name -- and Herb and myself, whatever restaurant we'd see, we'd go up and we'd stop.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Herb Jeffries as a star who frequented the jazz scene; the interviewee does not respond or provide personal interaction details.
